title: Optimization: skip tests in some circumstances
body:
A common pattern is to submit PRs that update only tests or docs.

When the only changes are to test/e2e, there is no point in running
test/system, and vice versa.

When the only changes are to docs, there is no point in running either.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: Can you do the same for just docs?  People often forget to add [CI:DOCS] when doing a docs only change. It would be a lot nicer if the system would figure this out.
/me thinks @rhatdan didn't read the code :wink: [edit: or the commit message]
Red but did not comprehend.  Mentally did an AND instead of an OR on the skip.

Thanks, @cevich. I too wish we could do this within `.cirrus.yml` but see no sane way to do it. Here we have an imperfect solution which still (sigh) spins up VMs but at least wastes much less developer and CPU time.

Re: `$CIRRUS_CHANGE_IN_REPO` - excellent point, thank you, I've fixed that logic.

> but see no sane way to do it.

Ya, it's just too complicated, and not growing any simpler.  There is a scripting language I've been thinking of that would make these things MUCH easier.  It can partly or fully take the place of `.cirrus.yml`, and supports actual/sane conditionals and looping.  [Docs ref.](https://cirrus-ci.org/guide/programming-tasks/) if your curious. 'Course would be a big migration and would break your cirrus task-map utility :cry: But it would bring some sanity to handling things like `CI:DOCS` and other conditions (like change-based skips) without so much brain-hurt.  Anyway, something I've been thinking of.

Validation: to test the logic, I snuck in a check-and-skip for `contrib` (like in this PR). [Cirrus results](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5281713126375424) confirm that pretty much everything got skipped as intended, you can verify by looking at all the `00:`, `01:`, and `02:` in the left-hand column. I clicked on a few of them and looked at `main`, confirmed that they truly say `Skipping`. I did not verify each individual one.

I've removed that `contrib` check (because I think we _should_ run full CI on changes therein), and have incorporated @cevich's excellent suggestions, and have removed the Draft status. I'm happy enough with this, and would appreciate final review.

(@rhatdan , @cevich, do note that I've added many more skip checks since the last time you may have looked).

Side-bar:  @edsantiago maybe after some soak-time, you'd consider moving `_only_changes_are_in()` into containers/automation common?  There's an opportunity to add some unit-tests there, but more importantly this function will be available for use in nearly all the other containers-org CI as well.

@cevich, good point about `hack`. I'll remove it -- no need to be overly aggressive from the start.

Re: `automation common`, my ~~thinking~~ waffling was that I would look into using `exit` from the helper itself, so we could do:
```diff
- if _only_changes_are_in this that & the other
- then
-     return
- fi
+ _bail_if_....
```
...which is so much cleaner and more readable. It just requires a lot of work for me to confirm that `exit` is 100% safe in this context. I'll do so this morning.

Major overhaul from yesterday: I rewrote it all in a much cleaner way. Verification: with the (temporary) addition of `contrib` to force-skip all skips, CI completed [in twenty minutes](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/6621563993718784) with lots of `Skip` messages. I've removed the `contrib`, and am happy with this, and it's ready for review.
Without the `contrib` kludge (i.e., as ready for review), CI finished in [ninety minutes](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5644873616326656) with no skips that I can see.

> ...which is so much cleaner and more readable. It just requires a lot of work for me to confirm that exit is 100% safe in this context. I'll do so this morning.

Ya I considered suggesting this, but was also nervous.  It's probably okay, since `runner.sh` is the final call for testing operations from `.cirrus.yml`.  Nothing ever follows it or depends on anything other than it exiting zero/non-zero to indicate status.
New push addresses your concerns: using `msg`, and updating the comments for clarity.
